Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 307 [Šumma Izbu 18-19][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] x KUN ⸢BABBAR ŠU⸣ MI TUR₃

(o 1) [...] tail is white: ... of the animal stall.

o 22

[...]-su BABBAR KUR [TUR]er ti-iṣ-bu-ti RI.RI.GA MIN<(TUR₃)>

(o 2) [...] its ... is white: the land will reduce; combat; epidemic of the animal stall.

o 33

[...] x x E₂? ⸢UZU?⸣ KUN BABBAR? ⸢EN?⸣ TUR₃ SIG₅

(o 3) [...] ... flesh of the tail is white: the animal stall's owner will prosper.

o 44

[...]-su UZU ina suq-ti-šu₂ e-li SAG.DU BABBAR KUN ⸢BABBAR EN TUR₃⸣ UŠ₂ [(x)] EGIR IRI SILIM.MA

(o 4) [...] its [...] flesh on its chin is whiter than the head (and its) tail is white: the animal stall's owner will die; afterwards, the city will be prosperous.

o 55

[...] x ⸢LA UZU ina suq-ti⸣-šu₂ e-li SAG.DU BABBAR KUN BABBAR EN TUR₃! UŠ₂ EGIR <DINGIR> IRI ḪUŠ

(o 5) [...] ... flesh on its chin is whiter than the head (and its) tail is white: the animal stall's owner will die; afterwards, the city's god will become angry.

o 66

[...]-⸢MEŠ-šu₂⸣ SAG.⸢DU!⸣ [x (x)] 2tu₄ ina SAG.KI-šu₂ GAL₂ GEŠTU kiš-šu₂-tu₂ ana? ⸢LUGAL? sa?⸣-li-mu ana EN TUR₃

(o 6) [...] its [...] the head (and) there are 2 [...] on its forehead: intelligence (and) authority for the king; peace for the animal stall's owner.

o 77

[...] x-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 IGI-MIN-šu₂ 2 KA-šu₂ 2 SI!-šu₂ šup-šu₂-ru šu₂-nu ana? x LUGAL NU x <<a-na>> ŠU-su KURad₂ ri-qu-su ana EN TUR₃

(o 7) [...] its [...] its eyes are 2, its mouths are 2, its horns are 2 (and) they are (all) very loose: the king (lit. his hand) will conquer the ...; emptiness for the animal stall's owner.

o 88

[... SAG].DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 KA-MEŠ-šu₂ 4 IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ eṣ-ṣe-en-ṣe-ri-šu₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-⸢šu₂ GIL?⸣ TI NUN x ⸢ri⸣-qu-su ana EN TUR₃

(o 8) [...] its heads are [...], its mouths are 2, its eyes are 4, (and) its 2 tails are crossed over its backbone: the prince's life ...; emptiness for the animal stall's owner.

o 99

[... SAG].DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 KA-MEŠ-[šu₂] 2 IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ 1 KUN-su pa?-ḫa-ar₂ tu-qu₂!?-un!?-tu₄ ⸢ri⸣-qu-su ana ⸢EN⸣ TUR₃1

(o 9) [...] its heads are [...], its mouths are 2, its eyes are 2, its tail is 1: gathering (for) battle; emptiness for the animal stall's owner.

o 1010

[...] x-MEŠ-šu₂ SAG.⸢DU⸣-MEŠ-[šu₂] x 3tu₄ ina SAG.⸢KI⸣-šu₂ x x LUGAL ri-qu-su x MA?

(o 10) [...] its [...], its heads ..., 3 ... on its forehead ...: ... of the king; emptiness for ....

o 1111

[...] AB₂ 1 NITA₂ 1 MUNUS U₅ x pag-ru [x (x)]

(o 11) [...] a cow ... 1 male, 1 female (and) (their) bodies are superimposed: [...].

o 1212

[...] x AB₂ 2 NITA₂ 1 MUNUS et-⸢gu?-ru?⸣ MU 1-KAM₂ ⸢EN⸣ [...]

(o 12) [...] ... a cow ... 2 males, 1 female are intertwined: for 1 year, the lord [...].

o 1313

[...] U₃.TE-ma 2 ⸢SAG.DU⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ ⸢LUGAL⸣ ZAḪ₂-⸢ma AŠ!(MU)TE MANni E₂ EN MAN-ma!(IS) TUKUši

(o 13) [...] gives birth and its heads are 2: the king will disappear and the throne will change; the house will acquire another owner.

o 1414

[...] ⸢U₃⸣.TE-ma 3 ⸢SAG.DU⸣-[MEŠ-šu₂] <<ana>> LUGAL ⸢ZI⸣bu ⸢ŠUB⸣

(o 14) [...] gives birth and its heads are 3: a rising will overpower the king.

o 1515

[BE] AB₂ 2 U₃.TE-ma ZA ⸢GIM?⸣ [...] ⸢UR?⸣ dam-ti::qa NUN BAL NUN IL₂a ⸢E₂ EN⸣ BIRaḫ

(o 15) [If] a cow gives birth to 2 and ... [...] ... good fortune for the prince; the prince's reign will be prominent; the owner's household will be scattered.

o 1616

BE AB₂ 3 ⸢U₃⸣.TE-<<ma>> UMUŠ KUR MANni

(o 16) If a cow gives birth to 3 (calves): decisions for the land will change.

o 1717

BE AB₂ ⸢U₃⸣.TE-ma i-pi₂ a-⸢ri⸣-im UMUŠ KUR MANni

(o 17) If a cow gives birth and (its calf) is covered with afterbirth: decisions for the land will change.

o 1818

BE AB₂ U₃.⸢TE⸣-ma ⸢i⸣-pi₂ ⸢a⸣-ri-im ⸢ŠA₃⸣.ḪUL EN DU₁₁-šu₂ GAZ!

(o 18) If a cow gives birth and (its calf) is covered with afterbirth: sorrow; his enemy will be killed.

o 1919

BE AB₂ ⸢U₃⸣.[TE-ma ...] x RI ina EME GAR [x] x x x x TUR₃ BI BIR⸢aḫ⸣

(o 19) If a cow gives birth and a [...] is located on (its) tongue: that animal stall will be scattered.

o 2020

[BE] ⸢AB₂⸣ [...] MUL-MEŠ ma-li ⸢TUR₃ EN?⸣ ar₂-ḫiš ⸢BIR⸣

(o 20) [If] a cow [...] (the calf) is covered with stars; its owner's animal stall will be quickly scattered.

o 2121

[...] ⸢U₃.TE⸣-[ma] GUN₃ GE₆ KI.TUŠ LUGAL! [...] ⸢DAGAL?⸣iš E₂.GAL BI ⸢ZAḪ₂⸣

(o 21) [...] gives birth [and] (a calf) is multicoloured, (a calf) is black: the king's residence [...] will expand; that palace will be destroyed.

o 2222

[...] U₃.TE-⸢ma⸣ 2 SI-MEŠ-šu₂ ina ZA₃ 1 ina ⸢GUB₃ LUGAL⸣ EN x x SU.GU₇

(o 22) [...] gives birth and (the calf has) 2 horns on the right, 1 on the left: the king will ... the lord; famine.

o 2323

[...] U₃.TE-ma 2 SI-MEŠ-šu₂ ina GUB₃ 1 ina ⸢ZA₃⸣ ZIut x x EN x

(o 23) [...] gives birth and (the calf has) 2 horns on the left, 1 on the right: attack ... the owner ....

o 2424

[...] ⸢U₃.TE⸣-ma 2 SI-MEŠ-šu₂ ina SAG.KI-šu₂ x A LUGAL-šu₂-nu [...] x DU₃⸢uš⸣ [...] x TUKUši

(o 24) [...] gives birth and (the calf has) 2 horns on its forehead: ... their king [...] will build ...; [...] will acquire ....

o 2525

[...]-⸢ma⸣ 2 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 GABA-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 x x-šu₂ 4 IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 KA-[MEŠ-šu₂ x] x-⸢MEŠ?⸣-šu₂ EŠ x [...] x d30 LUGAL DA KUR [...] SILIMmu ina KUR GAL₂ši

(o 25) [...] and its heads are 2, its chests are 2, its ... are 2, its eyes are 4, its mouths are 2 ..., its ... are [n]: ... [...] ...; Sin ... the king [...]; there will be peace in the land.

o 2626

[...] x-šu₂ up-pu-li TUR₃ BI [... E₂] NA BIR LU₂ ina KUR-⸢šu!(SU) x x i-tab-bal!

(o 26) [...] its ... is born late in season: that animal stall [...]; the man's household will be scattered; the man ... will take away ... from his land.

o 2727

[...] x ina SAG.KI-šu₂ U₄ [...] x KUR DU₃uš?

(o 27) [...] ... on its forehead ... will build [...] ... (for) the land.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] ZA₃ GIR₃-MIN x MAḪ [...]

(r 1) [...] on the right, the feet ... [...]

r 22

[...] GUB₃ GIR₃-MIN x MAḪ [...]

(r 2) [...] ... on the left, the feet ... [...]

r 33

[...] ⸢GIR₃⸣-[MIN ...] x ⸢LUGAL?⸣ giš⸢TUKUL?⸣-šu₂ UGU [...]

(r 3) [...] the feet [...] ...: as for the king, his army will [...] over [...].

r 44

[...]-MEŠ [x]-MEŠ-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN [...] gišTUKUL-MEŠ KUR₂ UGU [...]

(r 4) [...] ... its [...] the feet [...]: the enemy's armies will not [...] over [...].

r 55

[...] x GIR₃-MIN x [...]

(r 5) [...] ... the feet ... [...].

(rest of reverse blank)

1the reading for tuqunti is suggested after photographs, but remains tentative.
